justification: All pass criteria met: stripe wildcard identified as Critical, jsonwebtoken CVEs flagged as Critical with specific CVE numbers, all 5 of 5 risks identified (stripe, jsonwebtoken, left-pad, moment, crypto-random-string), safe_to_deploy is false, and severities are well-differentiated (Critical/High/Important/Suggestion levels).

observations.text: Stripe wildcard correctly identified as Critical with excellent explanation of why it's dangerous in a payment context
jsonwebtoken flagged with specific CVE numbers (CVE-2022-23529, CVE-2022-23539/23540/23541) and concrete exploit scenario
All 5 expected risks identified — stripe, jsonwebtoken, left-pad, crypto-random-string, and moment
crypto-random-string ESM issue correctly identified with appropriate caveat about CJS/ESM uncertainty
left-pad supply chain risk identified with historical context and actionable fix (padStart)
moment deprecation correctly noted at lower severity than the security issues
Bonus finding: missing package-lock.json risk, which compounds the stripe wildcard issue
safe_to_deploy is false with clear blocking conditions
Actionable fixes with time estimates for each finding

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: stripe wildcard identified as Critical; jsonwebtoken CVE flagged as Critical with specific CVE numbers; 5 of 5 risks identified (stripe, jsonwebtoken, left-pad, moment, crypto-random-string); safe_to_deploy false; severity ratings differentiated (Critical, High, Important, Suggestion levels).

observations.text: Stripe wildcard correctly identified as Critical with concrete risk explanation (breaking API changes, double charges, lost revenue)
jsonwebtoken CVEs cited by number (CVE-2022-23529, CVE-2022-23539/23540) with algorithm confusion attack vector explained
left-pad supply chain risk identified with historical context (2016 incident) and native replacement suggested (padStart)
crypto-random-string ESM-only issue caught and correctly noted as runtime breakage risk with CommonJS
moment.js deprecation identified with migration alternatives suggested
Bonus findings: missing engines field, no lockfile, no observability dependencies
Confidence section honestly notes low-confidence areas where source code would be needed to confirm findings

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: stripe wildcard identified as Critical with detailed reproduction steps; jsonwebtoken CVEs (CVE-2022-23529, CVE-2022-23539/23540/23541) flagged as Critical; 5 of 5 risks identified (stripe, jsonwebtoken, left-pad, moment, crypto-random-string); safe_to_deploy is false; severities are differentiated (Critical, High, Important, Suggestion levels).

observations.text: Stripe wildcard correctly identified as Critical with concrete reproduction scenario showing major version drift
jsonwebtoken CVEs cited by number (CVE-2022-23529, etc.) with specific upgrade path to >=9.0.0
crypto-random-string ESM/CJS incompatibility correctly identified with actionable fix (pin to v4.x or convert to ESM)
left-pad supply chain risk flagged with historical context and native replacement (padStart)
moment.js deprecation noted with alternatives suggested
Bonus findings: missing package-lock.json and overly wide pg range — adds value beyond the rubric
Confidence section transparently notes assumptions and low-confidence areas (ESM/CJS without seeing module config)
moment deprecation classified under important_issues rather than suggestions — rubric expected Suggestion severity, though this is minor
